MI6 / SIS — Secret Intelligence Service
Vauxhall Cross, London | Est. 1909
Britain's foreign intelligence service. Motto: "Semper Occultus" (Always Secret). Works alongside GCHQ (signals) and MI5 (domestic). Director known as "C". ~2,900 staff. Legal basis: Intelligence Services Act 1994. Operates covertly in 140+ countries. 5-eyes senior partner with NSA.
SIGINTHUMINTCovert ActionFive EyesSIS
GCHQ — Government Communications HQ
Cheltenham, UK | Est. 1919
TEMPORA: taps 200+ undersea fiber cables landing in UK, 21.6 Pbits/day. Rolling 30-day content buffer. JTRIG: online psychological operations — 4Ds (Deny, Disrupt, Degrade, Deceive). EncroChat 2020: decrypted 27M criminal messages, 800+ UK arrests.
► TEMPORA: 21.6 Pbits/day fiber collection
► JTRIG: Deny, Disrupt, Degrade, Deceive
► XKeyscore node: Bude Station Cornwall
► MUSCULAR: Google/Yahoo private cloud tap
GCHQSIGINTTEMPORAJTRIGFive Eyes
Notable Operations
Historical Record
VENONA (1943-1980): Joint US/UK decryption of Soviet comms — uncovered Cambridge Five (Philby, Burgess, Maclean, Blunt, Cairncross).

OPERATION FOOT (1971): Expelled 105 Soviet intelligence officers — largest peacetime intelligence expulsion ever.

ENCROCHAT (2020): French/UK joint op cracked EncroChat encrypted phone network. 60,000 users globally, 800+ UK arrests, £54M+ seized.

OPERATION GOLDENEYE (WWII): Ian Fleming's wartime mission planning. Inspiration for James Bond.
VENONAEncroChatCambridge FiveWWIIIran
NCSC — National Cyber Security Centre
GCHQ Subsidiary | Est. 2016
UK public face for cyber defence. Blocks 54M malicious domains/year via Active Cyber Defence. Mandatory 24h incident reporting for UK CNI. Co-publishes advisories with NSA/CISA/ASD on APT campaigns. GovAssure: cross-government cyber risk assessment.
► Active Cyber Defence: 54M domains blocked/year
► Protective DNS: 26M+ queries/day
► Joint advisories with NSA, CISA, ASD, CCCS
► GovAssure annual CNI cyber risk programme
NCSCGCHQUK CNIACDProtective DNS
Recruitment & Training Pipeline
GCHQ / MI6 | Classified
GCHQ CyberFirst: Government-run programme identifying cyber talent from age 14. Schools/colleges compete in national CTF. Top performers fast-tracked to GCHQ apprenticeships (starting £30k+). No degree required.

MI6 Application Process: "We recruit like any employer — except we also check if you've lived abroad, speak unusual languages, and can keep a secret." Multi-stage: psychometric tests, video interviews, assessment centres, SC/DV vetting (18+ months, full lifestyle polygraph).

JTRIG Training: GCHQ's Joint Threat Research Intelligence Group operatives train in: social media manipulation, building sock puppet networks, online persona management, disinformation techniques. Documented in Snowden files: "The Art of Deception" GCHQ training slides.

SANS / CREST Pathway: NCSC endorses CREST CRT, CCT qualifications. GCHQ Certified Training (GCT) scheme — only accredited courses recognised for CNI staff.
► CyberFirst: 4,000+ students/year in competition pipeline
► GCHQ apprenticeship: Level 4, starts at £30k, Cheltenham
► DV vetting: 18-month lifestyle investigation, foreign contacts checked
► NCSC CyberFirst Bursary: £4k/year + guaranteed GCHQ internship
GCHQMI6RecruitmentCyberFirstDV Clearance
JTRIG — Joint Threat Research Intelligence Group
GCHQ | Offensive Cyber Unit | TOP SECRET
GCHQ's offensive psychological operations and network exploitation unit. Revealed in Snowden leaks 2014. Core doctrine: 4Ds — Deny, Disrupt, Degrade, Deceive.

EFFECT Operations: "False flag" online, inject content into discussions, change photos on social media, post fabricated material to discredit. Target: terror networks, criminals, state actors.

CLEAN SWEEP: Masquerade as third-party to disrupt target. Use target's own infrastructure against them.

ROLLING THUNDER: DDoS capabilities against hacktivist groups (Anonymous, LulzSec). GCHQ targeted Anonymous IRC servers during Op Payback 2011.

GAMBITS FOR DECEPTION: Honey traps via fake online identities, compromising photos injected into target feeds, fake text messages to create mistrust between targets.
► JTRIG tools: GATEWAY (spoof SMS), POKERFACE (covert web browsing)
► AIRWOLF: targeted attack on Facebook, YouTube, Blogger accounts
► BADGER: mass delivery of targeted email campaigns
► WARPARTH: DDOS tool against target networks
► Snowden source: "The Art of Deception" 50-page training deck (2012)
JTRIGGCHQPsychological Ops4DsSnowden
GCHQ Technical Arsenal
CLASSIFIED // STRAP 3
TEMPORA: 200+ undersea cable tap points. Collects 21.6 Pbits/day. 30-day content buffer, 30-day metadata buffer (longer). Shared with NSA via MUSCULAR programme.

KARMA POLICE: Tracks every website visit of every visible internet user. Cross-references with MUTANT BROTH (correlates IP to web traffic) and MEMORY HOLE (Google search queries).

HACIENDA: Automated port scanning of 27 countries. Maps entire national internet infrastructure. Combined with SATANA for network device fingerprinting.

QUANTUMINSERT: Packet injection attack — intercepts target's HTTP request mid-transit and injects malicious response faster than the real server (race condition). Used to redirect targets to FOXACID exploit servers (shared with NSA). Bypasses need for any target action.

CNE (Computer Network Exploitation): GCHQ's own offensive malware teams mirror NSA TAO capabilities. Implants deployed against: Iranian nuclear programme, Al-Qaeda comms, Russian organised crime.
► QUANTUMINSERT: sub-50ms packet race vs legitimate HTTP server
► FOXACID: NSA/GCHQ shared exploit delivery platform (Metasploit-like)
► Bude Station (Cornwall): primary Atlantic cable collection point
► MUSCULAR: tapped Google/Yahoo internal datacenter links (unencrypted at time)
TEMPORAQUANTUMINSERTKARMA POLICEHACIENDAMUSCULAR
How to Hack Like GCHQ
Training Methodology | UNCLASSIFIED
Intelligence agencies don't just hire hackers — they build systematic training tracks:

Step 1 — Foundations: Networking (TCP/IP, DNS, TLS), OS internals (Windows NT architecture, Linux kernel), assembly language (x86/x64), Python scripting. All agencies require this baseline.

Step 2 — Offensive Track: Vulnerability research (fuzzing, source audit), exploit development (ROP chains, heap grooming, kernel pwn), malware development (implants, C2 frameworks, AV evasion), network intrusion (recon → initial access → persist → exfil).

Step 3 — Tradecraft: OPSEC (artefact minimisation, deniable infrastructure), attribution confusion (false-flag TTPs), HUMINT coordination with technical ops.

Civilian Pathway: GCHQ CyberFirst → NCSC apprenticeship → internal red team → CNE operators. Typical: 3-5 years to full offensive certification.

Competitions Used: DEF CON CTF, CSAW CTF, UCSB iCTF, internal GCHQ challenges. GCHQ publishes its own public CTF (GCHQ Christmas puzzle) annually to scout talent.
► HTB Pro Labs: GCHQ contractors train on Offshore, RastaLabs, Cybernetics
► SANS FOR508/SEC504/SEC660: GCHQ-endorsed red team path
► CRT → CCT: CREST certifications for UK CNI work
► Internal: GCHQ runs annual internal "Hack Week" red team exercises
TrainingCyberFirstCTFExploit DevGCHQ Careers
△ BND — BUNDESNACHRICHTENDIENST
// AGENCIES
BND Overview
BfV Domestic Intel
Operations & Scandals
BSI — Cyber Authority
// TRADECRAFT
ZiTiS — Hack Agency
German Cyber Ops
How Germany Trains
BND + NSA Scandal Deep Dive
BND — Bundesnachrichtendienst
Pullach → Berlin | Est. 1956
Germany's foreign intelligence service. Successor to the Gehlen Organisation (ex-Nazi intelligence recruited by CIA post-WWII). 6,500 employees, budget ~€920M. Partners with Five Eyes. Criticised for NSA Selektoren affair — used NSA-supplied selectors to spy on EU partners including France, Airbus, EU Commission 2013-2015.
GermanyBNDSIGINTNATOEU Intelligence
BfV — Verfassungsschutz
Federal Office for the Protection of the Constitution | Domestic
Germany's domestic intelligence — equivalent to MI5. Monitors extremism, foreign espionage, and cyber attacks. BfV vs BND: domestic vs foreign. Scandal: BfV worked with NSA to spy on EU partners 2013-2015 using 40,000+ NSA "selectors" (phone numbers, email addresses of EU officials, Airbus engineers).
BfVDomesticCounter-EspionageGermany
Notable Operations & Scandals
Historical Intelligence
GEHLEN ORG (1946-1956): CIA recruited Nazi general Reinhard Gehlen. Built West German intel using wartime Soviet agent networks. Later became BND.

NSA SELEKTOREN AFFAIR (2013): BND forwarded NSA selectors targeting EU Commission, Airbus, French ministry. Largest German intelligence scandal in postwar history. Parliamentary inquiry 2015-2017.

BUNDESTAG HACK (2015): APT28 (GRU) compromised Bundestag IT for weeks. 16GB data exfiltrated including Merkel emails. BfV attributed to Russia.
GehlenNSA AffairAPT28BundestagAirbus
BSI — Federal Office for Information Security
Bundesamt für Sicherheit in der Informationstechnik
Germany's national cybersecurity authority — equivalent to UK NCSC. BSI Act 2021: mandatory 24h CNI incident reporting. Runs CERT-Bund. Issues mandatory KRITIS security standards. Infamously recommended Kaspersky products until Russian invasion of Ukraine 2022, then immediately warned all German organisations to remove them.
► KRITIS: mandatory standards for critical infrastructure
► CERT-Bund: 24h CNI incident reporting
► BSI-Grundschutz: baseline security catalogue
► Kaspersky warning issued 2022-03-15
BSIKRITISCERT-BundGermanyStandards
ZiTiS — Zentralstelle für Informationstechnik im Sicherheitsbereich
Munich | Est. 2017 | ~400 staff
Germany's secret hacking agency — the closest equivalent to NSA's TAO or GCHQ's CNE team. Created after public outrage over NSA surveillance to give Germany its own offensive capability.

Mission: Develop Staatstrojaner (state trojans) for lawful interception. Provide technical capabilities to BKA (Federal Criminal Police), BfV (domestic intel), BND (foreign intel), and customs.

Staatstrojaner: Court-ordered phone/PC malware for wiretaps. Commercial: FinFisher (Munich-based) until 2021, then in-house development. Controversy: same technology used by authoritarian regimes.

Quantum Capabilities: ZiTiS budget for quantum decryption research — attempting to break TLS/VPN/PGP at scale. Post-quantum cryptography migration now mandatory for German federal agencies.

Recruiting: ZiTiS openly recruits at CCC (Chaos Communication Congress) — Germany's hacker conference — unusual for an intelligence agency.
► Budget: €60M/year (2024 estimate, classified in detail)
► Staatstrojaner: must target specific individuals by court order
► FinFisher GmbH Munich shut down 2021 after civil society pressure
► ZiTiS job ads: Python, C++, RE, pentest — posted on regular job boards
► CCC cooperation: controversial — hackers vs. surveillance state
ZiTiSStaatstrojanerGermanyOffensive CyberLawful Intercept
German Offensive Cyber Operations
BND / ZiTiS | CLASSIFIED
HADES (suspected): BND-linked SIGINT programme intercepting satellite communications of partner nations. European version of NSA UPSTREAM. Exposed via Snowden docs — BND collected EU government comms using NSA selectors.

KSN — Cyber Defence Command: Bundeswehr's military cyber unit. 14,000 IT/cyber personnel. Offensive mandate since 2021. Conducted first acknowledged offensive operation: disrupted Russian troll farm infrastructure during 2021 Bundestag election period.

Telekom CERT: Deutsche Telekom honey pot network — 5,000 sensors globally catching 40M+ attacks/day. Shared with BSI, CERT-EU, Europol.

Operation TRIANGULATION attribution: BfV co-attributed 2023 iPhone zero-click (TriangleDB) to FSB. Kaspersky researchers found it — BND noted Kaspersky HQ Moscow proximity created conflict of interest.
► KSN Kommando Strategische Aufklärung: SIGINT + offensive since 2017
► Cyber domain = 6th domain of warfare (added to Bundeswehr doctrine 2016)
► BND Technik: intercepts approx. 15% of Germany's international internet traffic
► Operation Rubikon: BND + CIA secretly owned Crypto AG — sold rigged encryption to 130 nations
BND CyberKSNBundeswehrSIGINTCrypto AG
How Germany Trains Cyber Operators
BSI / KSN / ZiTiS Pathways
Academic Pipeline: MINT subjects (Math, IT, Natural Science, Technology) prioritised. Key universities: TU Munich, KIT Karlsruhe, TU Berlin, Saarland University (CISPA — Germany's top security research institute).

CISPA Helmholtz Center: Federal-funded security research. Publications cover: fuzzing, memory safety, cryptanalysis, malware detection. Talent pipeline to BSI/BND/ZiTiS.

Chaos Communication Congress (CCC): Annual Hamburg conference, 15,000 attendees. Ethical hacker culture. BSI and ZiTiS recruiters attend. CCC's own competition: CTF events at 37C3, 38C3.

Military Path: Bundeswehr University Munich — cyber specialisation. KSN direct recruiting from Computer Science graduates. Internal Red Team training: 6-month offensive course.

BSI IT-Grundschutz Pathway: Civilian route: IT-Grundschutz practitioner → BSI analyst → offensive research. Entry-level: OSCP equivalent, then BSI-internal advanced courses.
► CISPA: €50M/year federal research budget, 400+ researchers
► CCC CTF: 300+ team international competition, German teams top-ranked
► BSI hiring: German citizenship mandatory, extended background check
► KSN: 6-month Red Team Course, Bundeswehr internal, no public curriculum
GermanyTrainingCISPACCCBundeswehr
BND + NSA Scandal — Full Timeline
Selektoren Affair | Parliamentary Inquiry 2015-2017
The most detailed public exposure of a "Five Eyes-adjacent" agency actively spying on its own EU partners using US-supplied targeting intelligence.

Background: Post-9/11, BND agreed to share SIGINT with NSA. NSA provided "selectors" — phone numbers, email addresses, keywords — to BND for collection against BND's own SIGINT infrastructure (satellite intercepts, cable taps).

The Problem: NSA selectors included: EU Commission officials, French foreign ministry, Austrian government, EADS/Airbus engineers (US aerospace competitor intel). BND collected on behalf of NSA against Germany's own EU allies.

Scale: 40,000+ NSA selectors used by BND. 2,000 flagged as problematic by BND's own analysis but passed on anyway. Total collection: billions of metadata records/day shared via SIGDASYS.

Aftermath: Parliamentary inquiry 2015-2017. Chancellor Merkel emails confirmed as NSA target. New BND Act 2021: restricts collection against EU/NATO partners. Relations with NSA formally restructured.
► Selectors: 40,000+ NSA-supplied targeting identifiers
► Targets: Airbus, EU Commission, French foreign ministry, Austria
► Merkel's mobile: listed in NSA target database (separate from BND affair)
► Crypto AG (Operation Rubikon): BND/CIA owned Swiss firm 1970-1993, sold rigged encryption to 130 countries
NSABNDSelektorenEU SpyingParliamentary Inquiry

MOSSAD — Institute for Intelligence
Tel Aviv | Est. 1949
Israel's national intelligence agency. Motto: "By Way of Deception, Thou Shalt Do War." ~7,000 employees. Reports directly to Prime Minister. Primary mission: foreign intelligence, counterterrorism, covert operations. Alongside: Shin Bet (internal security), Aman (military intelligence), Unit 8200 (SIGINT/cyber). NSO Group spyware (Pegasus) built by Unit 8200 alumni.
IsraelMossadHUMINTCovertUnit 8200
Unit 8200 — SIGINT / Elite Cyber
IDF Intelligence Corps
World's most elite cyber military unit. Former members founded Check Point, CyberArk, Palo Alto Networks, Armis, Illusive Networks, and 3,000+ security companies — making Israel a global cyber superpower. Co-developed Stuxnet with NSA. NSO Group (Pegasus spyware) founded by alumni. Israel exports offensive cyber capabilities to 100+ countries through 8200 alumni startups.
► Co-developed Stuxnet with NSA (Olympic Games 2007-2010)
► Alumni companies: NSO, CheckPoint, CyberArk, Palo Alto
► OP ORCHARD 2007: cyber-blind Syrian radar (IAF airstrike)
► Duqu, Flame malware — strongly suspected (unconfirmed)
Unit 8200StuxnetNSOCyberSIGINT
Famous Operations
Declassified / Historical
EICHMANN (1960): Kidnapped Nazi war criminal Adolf Eichmann from Buenos Aires. First use of intel extraction operation at this scale.

WRATH OF GOD (1972-88): Assassinations of Black September operatives after Munich Olympics massacre. Depicted in Spielberg's "Munich."

OPERATION PLUMBAT (1968): Hijacked 200 tons of uranium yellowcake from a ship in the Mediterranean for Israel's undeclared nuclear programme.

OPERATION ORCHARD (2007): IAF destroyed Syrian nuclear reactor. Unit 8200 blinded Syrian air defences — planes never appeared on radar.

STUXNET (2007-2010): Destroyed 1,000+ Iranian centrifuges at Natanz. Set enrichment back 2 years.
EichmannMunichStuxnetSyriaNuclear
Iran vs Israel — Cyberwar
Ongoing Conflict
NUCLEAR SCIENTISTS (2010-2012): Series of assassinations of Iranian nuclear scientists in Tehran attributed to Mossad.

ATOMIC ARCHIVE (2018): Mossad extracted 110,000 files from Iranian nuclear archive in Tehran — confirmed Iran's covert weapons programme detail.

NATANZ POWER (2021): Explosion at Natanz facility — attributed to Israel, destroyed centrifuge production capability.

IRANIAN CYBER RETALIATION: Iran-linked APT groups (APT35/Charming Kitten) conduct phishing against Israeli defence, research, and civil targets continuously.
IranNuclearNatanzCyberIntelligence
NSO Group — Pegasus Spyware
Unit 8200 Alumni | Herzliya, Israel
What Is Pegasus: Mobile surveillance implant targeting iOS and Android. Sold to governments exclusively. Capabilities: full call interception, SMS/WhatsApp read, camera/microphone activation, GPS tracking, email access — all covertly, zero user interaction required with latest zero-click exploits.

Zero-Click Exploit Chain: NSO used: KISMET (iMessage, 2019 — parse malicious GIF), FORCEDENTRY (JBIG2 decoder integer overflow, CVE-2021-30860, 2021), BlastPass (PassKit, 2023). Each exploits parsing of media/attachments without any user tap. Apple patches within days; NSO finds new chains within weeks.

Victims: 50,000 phone numbers in leaked list. Confirmed: journalists (Khashoggi's associates), heads of state (Macron, Modi), activists, lawyers. Sold to: UAE, Saudi Arabia, India, Mexico, Rwanda, Hungary, Azerbaijan.

Technical Detection: Citizen Lab (Toronto) pioneered forensic detection via iMazing backup analysis. Indicators: DataUsage.sqlite anomalies, process crash logs (com.apple.mediaserverd), network connections to Pegasus infrastructure domains.
► FORCEDENTRY: JBIG2 decoder vuln — used against activist Monika Cyril's iPhone
► Khashoggi: 5 of his associates targeted before assassination (Oct 2018)
► Apple Threat Notification: alerts users when state-sponsored spyware suspected
► NSO valuation (2021): $1B. Blacklisted by US Commerce Dept Nov 2021.
► Detection: MVT (Mobile Verification Toolkit) — open source, iOS/Android
NSOPegasusZero-ClickiOSFORCEDENTRY
Operation: Pager Attack — September 2024
Hezbollah Supply Chain | Lebanon
One of the most audacious supply chain operations in intelligence history. Israel (widely attributed to Mossad/IDF) compromised the supply chain of AR-924 pager devices ordered by Hezbollah.

Method: PETN explosive (~10g per device) planted in battery compartment during manufacturing or shipping. When Hezbollah activated all devices simultaneously (code word sent via pager network), devices detonated across Lebanon on 17 September 2024.

Scale: ~3,000 pagers detonated. 12 killed, 2,800+ wounded. Next day: Iridium-brand walkie-talkie radios detonated (separate batch, same supply chain operation). Hezbollah communications infrastructure paralysed.

Supply Chain Details: Pagers were AR-924 model from BAC Consulting (Budapest front company, actually Mossad-linked). Hezbollah ordered to avoid Israeli intercept of smartphones. The counter-surveillance measure became the attack vector.

Intelligence Significance: Demonstrates that supply chain attacks cross from cyber into physical domain. Same principles as SolarWinds but with kinetic payload. Hardware implants (NSA ANT catalogue) predicted this convergence.
► Date: 17-18 September 2024 — two-day operation
► Pager model: GOLD APOLLO AR-924 — Taiwanese brand, Hungarian distributor
► PETN: 10-20g per device, enough to injure/kill at close range
► Signal: Hezbollah believed pagers were NSA/Mossad-proof vs smartphones
► Attribution: Israel did not confirm/deny. US briefed in advance per reports.
MossadSupply ChainLebanonHezbollahPager Attack 2024
Unit 8200 — Training & Career Pipeline
IDF Intelligence Corps | Most Selective Unit
Selection: Drafted at 18. Psychometric testing, aptitude tests in math/logic/computing. Top 1-2% of eligible draftees. Girls and boys equally represented — Israel's mandatory service applies to both.

Training Tracks:
• SIGINT Analysts: signals collection, decryption, language analysis
• Software/Malware Developers: write offensive tools, implants, C2 frameworks
• Network Operators: active intrusion, CNE (Computer Network Exploitation)
• Data Science/AI: processing collected intelligence at scale

Technical Curriculum (estimated from alumni accounts): C/C++/Python, assembly, RE (IDA Pro, Ghidra), exploit development, network protocol analysis, firmware reverse engineering. Real-world targets from day one — no sandbox environment.

Post-Service: 3 years mandatory. Alumni: 90% found companies or join top tech. Check Point (firewall), CyberArk (PAM), Armis (IoT), Illusive (deception), Wiz (cloud security), ClearSky, Team8 VC. Israel has highest number of cybersecurity unicorns per capita globally.

Elite Within Elite: 81 Unit — SIGINT collection. Sayeret Matkal — cyber used in conjunction with direct action. These units share personnel with 8200.
► 8200 alumni companies: 3,000+ including 30+ unicorns ($1B+ valuation)
► Israeli cybersecurity exports: $3.38B (2022) — 10% of global market
► Female officers: ~40% of Unit 8200 (unusual vs other military cyber units)
► 8200 Alumni Association: 15,000 members, acts as startup accelerator
Unit 8200TrainingIsraelAlumniCybersecurity
Flame / Duqu / Gauss — Israeli Cyber Weapons
2011-2012 Discovery | Highly Sophisticated
Three related malware families discovered 2011-2012, strongly attributed to Unit 8200 / NSA (Olympic Games programme extension).

FLAME (Flamer/sKyWIper): 20MB — largest malware ever discovered at time. Modular platform: 20 plugins including: audio recording, screenshot capture, Bluetooth device scanning, keylogging, network sniffing, covert C2 via Bluetooth. Targeted Iran, Lebanon, Syria, Sudan. Used MD5 collision attack to fake Windows Update certificate (first real-world MD5 collision exploit).

DUQU: Stuxnet codebase reuse. Keylogger/data exfil for intelligence gathering prior to sabotage ops. Found on European industrial software vendor systems — assessing Stuxnet effectiveness.

GAUSS: Banking trojan hybrid. Targeted Lebanese banks — likely mapping Hezbollah financial flows. Encrypted payload (Palida Narrow font anomaly) never decrypted. Used USB spreading like Stuxnet.

Why It Matters: Shows intelligence agencies build full malware platforms with years-long development cycles. Not single exploits — entire frameworks with plugin systems, encrypted C2, persistence, self-destruct triggers.
► Flame: MD5 collision for fake Windows Update cert — first in-the-wild use
► Duqu: Used zero-day kernel vuln (Win32k.sys TTF font parser, CVE-2011-3402)
► Gauss: Palida Narrow font used to fingerprint target PCs before delivery
► Flame C2: used Bluetooth for covert comms when no internet connectivity
► Self-destruct: Flame issued "SUICIDE" command to all instances post-discovery
FlameDuquGaussUnit 8200Cyber Weapons

MSS — Ministry of State Security
Beijing | Est. 1983
China's combined foreign intelligence / domestic counterintelligence agency. Combines CIA + FBI functions. ~100,000+ employees, 17 provincial bureaus. Primary cyber through Technical Reconnaissance Bureaus (TRBs) and front companies (Chengdu 404, I-Soon/Anxun). PLA SSF runs military cyber: Unit 61398, Unit 61419. Economic espionage is primary goal: steal decades of R&D in months.
MSSChinaAPTEspionagePLA
Chinese Cyber Units — APT Structure
PLA / MSS Organisation
APT1 / PLA Unit 61398: Shanghai. First publicly named (Mandiant 2013). 141 companies in 20 industries. Terabytes of IP stolen from aerospace/energy/IT.

APT10 / Stone Panda: MSP intrusions — accessed hundreds of companies through trusted IT providers. US/UK/AUS/Japan indictments 2018.

APT40 / TEMP.Periscope: Hainan province MSS. Maritime/naval focus. OPM breach 2015: 21.5M US fed employee clearance files stolen.

APT41 / Double Dragon: MSS contractor Chengdu 404. Dual espionage + financial crime. 14 countries, healthcare/gaming/telco/govt.
APT1APT10APT40APT41PLA 61398
Major Chinese Cyber Operations
Historical Attribution
OPM BREACH (2014-2015): APT40 stole 21.5M US federal employees' SF-86 clearance forms. Every government employee who applied for clearance since 1985. Catastrophic for CIA overseas operations.

HAFNIUM / EXCHANGE (2021): Chinese actors exploited 4 MS Exchange zero-days (CVE-2021-26855 etc) — 250,000+ servers worldwide, including NATO defence and European banking sector.

VOLT TYPHOON (2023-present): Pre-positioning in US water/energy/comms/military logistics. LOLBins only. FBI: "Preparing for cyberattacks in event of Taiwan conflict."
OPMExchangeVolt TyphoonTaiwanCritical Infra
I-Soon Contractor Leak — 2024
GitHub: 570MB internal documents
February 2024: unknown actor uploaded I-Soon (Anxun Information Technology) internal documents to GitHub. I-Soon = Shanghai MSS contractor. Contents revealed:
► APT-for-hire tooling: Twitter/X, Telegram, email compromise tools
► Client list: Chinese govt agencies, provincial Public Security Bureaus
► Target list: 20+ foreign govts, militaries, NGOs across SE Asia/Europe/US
► Employee salaries: $900-$1,400/month for hackers
► Internal complaints about low pay, poor management, burnout
I-SoonMSSContractorLeak2024
Huawei / TikTok — Embedded Collection
Tech as Intelligence Infrastructure
China's Belt and Road Initiative (BRI) in 150+ countries serves dual purpose: economic influence and passive intelligence collection. Huawei network equipment in 170+ countries; US/UK/EU/AUS ban for CNI. TikTok (ByteDance) mandatory data sharing with Chinese parent — US ban 2024. China's "Cyber Sovereignty" doctrine: the internet is a weapon of state power.
HuaweiTikTokBRIEconomic EspionageBelt and Road
Salt Typhoon — 2024 Telecom Breach
PRC APT | US Carriers | ACTIVE
The largest known breach of US telecommunications infrastructure. Discovered late 2024, active for 12-18 months prior. Attributed to PRC-linked threat actor "Salt Typhoon" (Microsoft naming).

What Was Breached: At least 9 major US telecoms including AT&T, Verizon, T-Mobile, Lumen. Also Canada, UK, Australia telecoms affected. Targeted CALEA (Communications Assistance for Law Enforcement Act) lawful intercept infrastructure — the wiretap portals built for US law enforcement.

What Was Stolen: Call metadata (who called whom, when, location). Audio of calls from senior US government officials and political figures. List of all people under US government surveillance warrant — entire intelligence community targeting list.

Technical Method: Exploited Cisco IOS vulnerabilities in border routers. Moved laterally to CALEA infrastructure. Maintained persistence 12-18 months. Blended with legitimate network management traffic.

Impact: FBI/CISA issued unprecedented recommendation: use end-to-end encrypted apps (Signal) instead of traditional phone calls. China now knows every US intelligence target for that period.
► Carriers confirmed: AT&T, Verizon, T-Mobile, Lumen, Charter, Consolidated
► CALEA access: lawful intercept backdoor turned against the builders
► Duration: ~18 months undetected in core telecom infrastructure
► FBI guidance: switch to E2E encrypted comms — first such public recommendation
► Cisco vulns: CVE-2023-20198, CVE-2023-20273 — IOS-XE exploitation chain
Salt TyphoonChinaTelecomsCALEA2024
How China Trains State Hackers
PLA SSF / MSS / Contractor Pipeline
University Pipeline: PLA Information Engineering University (Zhengzhou) — primary military cyber school. 10,000+ students. Curriculum: signals, cryptography, network warfare, EW. Direct pipeline to PLA SSF (Strategic Support Force) cyber units.

CTF Culture: China has the world's most competitive CTF ecosystem. XCTF League, TCTF (Tencent), BCTF. Teams like r3kapig, Nu1L, AAA dominate internationally. Many top CTF players recruited directly by MSS contractors.

Contractor-to-APT Pipeline: I-Soon leak reveals: private companies (Chengdu 404, I-Soon, CNCERT contractors) hired by provincial MSS bureaus. Hackers recruited via CTF rankings, Weibo hacker forums, and university career fairs. Salary: ¥6,000-¥10,000/month ($800-$1,400) for active operators.

Patriotic Hacker Programmes: CNVD (China National Vulnerability Database) rewards — domestic researchers submit to CNVD before CVE. State can exploit before patch. "National Cybersecurity Awareness Week" — government-run competitions recruits talent.

PLA SSF Track: Gaokao top scorers → PLA university → 2-year technical training → unit assignment. Elite: selected for "Blue Team" (offensive), rest go to "Red Team" (actually defensive in Chinese military naming — inverted from Western convention).
► PLA IEU Zhengzhou: 7 campuses, classified curriculum, direct SSF pipeline
► CNVD: bugs submitted here stay 0-day in Chinese hands for average 68 days
► I-Soon leak: hackers complained about 996 (9am-9pm 6 days), low pay, poor management
► CTF-to-APT: r3kapig members identified in APT attribution reports
► Tencent, Alibaba, Huawei: mandated to share all discovered vulns with MSS
ChinaTrainingCTFPLA SSFMSS Contractors
Chinese APT TTPs — Deep Technical Dive
MITRE ATT&CK | PRC Techniques
Living off the Land (LOTL): Volt Typhoon signature technique. Use built-in Windows tools only: wmic, netsh, ntdsutil, PowerShell, certutil. No custom malware dropped — EDR has nothing to detect. LOLBAS technique maximised.

Web Shell Preference: APT40 consistently plants web shells (ASPX/PHP) on Exchange, SharePoint, Confluence after initial exploit. ChinaChopper web shell — 4KB, evades AV, persistent. DEWMODE (HAFNIUM), ANTSWORD variants.

VPN Appliance Targeting: 2023-2024: Ivanti, Fortinet, Citrix, Pulse Secure all hit by Chinese-linked 0-days. Reason: VPN appliances run on internet edge, often excluded from EDR coverage, admin credentials give domain access immediately.

Credential Theft Scale: OPM 2015 — SF-86 forms contained: childhood addresses, foreign contacts, financial history, medical history, drug use, family members. Perfect for blackmail, false legend creation, identifying vulnerable officials.

Strategic Patience: Chinese APTs maintain access for months-years before exfil. APT10 in MSPs: 18 months avg dwell time. Volt Typhoon: pre-positioning only, no current exfil observed — infrastructure for future kinetic-cyber coordination.
► LOTL: Volt Typhoon found in US infra using only built-in tools — no malware
► VPN 0-days: Ivanti CVE-2024-21887+CVE-2023-46805 chained — 2,100 devices day 1
► APT41: average 17 DAYS from public CVE disclosure to exploitation in the wild
► ChinaChopper: ~4KB web shell, 10+ years continuous use across multiple APT groups
► Dwell time: Chinese APTs average 287 days in network before detection (Mandiant)
Volt TyphoonLOTLWeb ShellVPN 0-dayCredential Theft
MSS Contractor Economy — APT-for-Hire
I-Soon / Chengdu 404 / CNCERT Shell Companies
China's offensive cyber is outsourced to a network of private "information security" companies contracted by provincial and national MSS bureaus. Cheaper, deniable, scalable.

How It Works: MSS bureau issues task order (target list, collection requirements). Contractor assigns operators. Contractor delivers exfiltrated data. MSS pays per successful breach. Contractors compete for contracts — creating financial incentive to hack more targets.

Chengdu 404: APT41's corporate front. Seven employees indicted by US DOJ 2020. Legally registered IT security company in Chengdu. Customers included Chinese government and criminal clients simultaneously. Shows dual espionage+criminal use.

I-Soon Leak Details: Internal documents show: product catalogue with prices (Twitter account compromise: ¥10,000; mailbox: ¥6,000; penetration of a government: varies). Customer satisfaction ratings. Internal Slack-like messages complaining about client not paying invoices.

Contractor Quality Tiers: Tier 1 (e.g. PLA 61398 Unit): full-time military, best tooling. Tier 2 (MSS contractors): good but mercenary. Tier 3 (patriotic freelancers): opportunistic, noisy, less disciplined — harder to control.
► Chengdu 404: 7 employees indicted DOJ Sep 2020, company still operating
► I-Soon price list: infiltrate foreign ministry = negotiable; email = ¥6,000 (~$830)
► APT41 operated 2012-2020 documented — financial crime concurrent with espionage
► MSS contractor count: estimated 50-100 companies in China hack-for-hire ecosystem
► US sanctions: Chengdu 404, CASTIC, Huaying Haitai named in DOJ/OFAC actions
I-SoonChengdu 404MSSContractorsAPT Economy
◆ DGSE — DIRECTION GÉNÉRALE DE LA SÉCURITÉ EXTÉRIEURE
// FRANCE
DGSE Overview
ANSSI Cyber Authority
